perturbation.original_text: VII. ATTORNEY'S FEES: If any legal action is brought by any party hereto, it is expressly agreed that the prevailing party in such action or dispute shall be entitled to recover from the other party attorney's fees and costs in addition to any other relief that may be awarded. For the purpose of this Paragraph, the "prevailing party" shall be the party in whose favor a final judgment is entered.
perturbation.changed_text:
I.DEFINITIONS:

VII. ATTORNEY'S FEES: If any legal action is brought by any party hereto, it is expressly agreed that the prevailing party in such action or dispute shall be entitled to recover from the other party attorney's fees and costs in addition to any other relief that may be awarded. For the purpose of this Paragraph, the "prevailing party" shall be the party in whose favor a final judgment is entered.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the 'Attorney's Fees' clause into the 'Definitions' section, which mainly defines key terms, makes it less likely to be noticed by someone looking for fee-related information. This obscurement could prevent a party from fully understanding their financial responsibilities in case of a legal dispute.
perturbation.law_explanation: Missouri's Merchandising Practices Act aims to protect consumers from unfair or deceptive practices, which includes ensuring clarity in contracts. Hiding the attorney's fees clause within the definitions undermines transparency, potentially deceiving consumers about the financial risks associated with disputes and thus could be seen as a violation of § 407.020.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Missouri Revisor of Statutes - Revised Statutes of Missouri, RSMo Section 407.020 ☰ Revisor of Missouri Constitution Committee Publications Other Links Help / FAQ Appendices and Tables Words Section None Or And Not Near Do search Help This chapter only Title XXVI TRADE AND COMMERCE Chapter 407 < > • Effective - 28 Aug 2020, 5 histories 407.020. Unlawful practices, penalty — exceptions. — 1. The act, use or employment by any person of any deception, fraud, false pretense, false promise, misrepresentation, unfair practice or the concealment, suppression, or omission of any material fact in connection with the sale or advertisement of any merchandise in trade or commerce or the solicitation of any funds for any charitable purpose, as defined in section 407.453 , in or from the state of Missouri, is declared to be an unlawful practice. The use by any person, in connection with the sale or advertisement of any merchandise in trade or commerce or the solicitation of any funds for any charitable purpose, as defined in section 407.453 , in or from the state of Missouri of the fact that the attorney general has approved any filing required by this chapter as the approval, sanction or endorsement of any activity, project or action of such person, is declared to be an unlawful practice. Any act, use or employment declared unlawful by this subsection violates this subsection whether committed before, during or after the sale, advertisement or solicitation. 2. Nothing contained in this section shall apply to: (1) The owner or publisher of any newspaper, magazine, publication or printed matter wherein such advertisement appears, or the owner or operator of a radio or television station which disseminates such advertisement when the owner, publisher or operator has no knowledge of the intent, design or purpose of the advertiser; (2) Any institution, company, or entity that is subject to chartering, licensing, or regulation by the director of the department of commerce and insurance under chapter 354 or chapters 374 to 385 , the director of the division of cred
perturbation.justification: The modification creates a structural flaw by moving the attorney's fees clause into the definitions section. This makes the clause less visible and accessible, potentially misleading a party about their financial responsibilities. According to the Law Explanation, this undermines transparency and could deceive consumers about the financial risks associated with disputes. Scraped Law Snippet 1 defines unlawful practice as including 'the concealment, suppression, or omission of any material fact in connection with the sale or advertisement of any merchandise in trade or commerce'. While the contract isn't directly related to sale of merchandise it does involve trade, and hiding the fees clause could be interpreted as a form of concealment of a material fact related to the agreement, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: XII.EQUITABLE RELIEF : Each party recognizes that any actual or threatened breach of the provisions of this Agreement would cause irreparable harm to the other party, the extent of which would be difficult and impracticable to assess, and that money damages would not be an adequate remedy for such breach. Accordingly, in addition to and not in limitation of any other remedies at law or in equity available under the circumstances, and notwithstanding any jurisdiction and venue requirements of this Agreement, each party shall be entitled to seek immediate injunctive and other non -monetary equitable relief in any court of competent jurisdiction without the need or requirement of any bond, provided that if a bond is required, the bond shall not exceed $1,000.00.
perturbation.changed_text:
VI.NO COMMITMENT; NO LI CENSE; NO WARRANTY : Neither party shall be obligated under this Agreement to commence or continue with any Discussions, enter into any particular Business Relationship, or disclose any particular Confidential Information to the other party. No express or implied license under any intellectual property rights of either party is granted or conveyed to the other party under this Agreement. ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PRO VIDED UNDER THIS AGREEMENT “AS IS” WITHOUT WARRANTIES OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, AND DISCLOSER DISCLAIMS ANY IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE AND NON -INFRINGEMENT.

XII.EQUITABLE RELIEF : Each party recognizes that any actual or threatened breach of the provisions of this Agreement would cause irreparable harm to the other party, the extent of which would be difficult and impracticable to assess, and that money damages would not be an adequate remedy for such breach. Accordingly, in addition to and not in limitation of any other remedies at law or in equity available under the circumstances, and notwithstanding any jurisdiction and venue requirements of this Agreement, each party shall be entitled to seek immediate injunctive and other non -monetary equitable relief in any court of competent jurisdiction without the need or requirement of any bond, provided that if a bond is required, the bond shall not exceed $1,000.00.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the 'Equitable Relief' clause, which discusses remedies for breach, into the 'No Commitment; No License; No Warranty' section, which primarily focuses on the scope and limitations of the agreement, dilutes its impact. This obscurement could make it harder for a party to understand their rights to seek injunctive relief in case of a breach.
perturbation.law_explanation: Missouri law requires contracts to be interpreted fairly and reasonably, considering the intent of the parties. By placing the 'Equitable Relief' clause in a section about warranty disclaimers, the modification obscures the intent and the legal remedies available, making it more difficult to enforce the equitable relief provisions as intended by the parties and thus is against the general spirit of § 431.080 to interpret contracts reasonably and fairly.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Missouri Revisor of Statutes - Revised Statutes of Missouri, RSMo Section 431.080 ☰ Revisor of Missouri Constitution Committee Publications Other Links Help / FAQ Appendices and Tables Words Section None Or And Not Near Do search Help This chapter only Title XXVIII CONTRACTS AND CONTRACTUAL RELATIONS Chapter 431 < > Effective - 28 Aug 1939 431.080. Suit may be instituted thereon. — Suits may be commenced and prosecuted thereon in the name of such county, or in the name of the person to whom they are made, to the use of the county, as fully and effectually as any person may or can, upon like notes, bills, bonds, contracts, agreements or writings made to him. ­­-------- (RSMo 1939 § 13765) Prior revisions: 1929 § 12106; 1919 § 9504; 1909 § 3725 ---- end of effective 28 Aug 1939 ---- use this link to bookmark section 431.080 Effective dates prior to 1940 may not be the actual effective date. perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that moving the 'Equitable Relief' clause into the 'No Commitment; No License; No Warranty' section dilutes its impact and obscures the intent of the parties. The law explanation provided states that Missouri law requires contracts to be interpreted fairly and reasonably, considering the intent of the parties, and that the modification obscures the legal remedies available, making it more difficult to enforce the equitable relief provisions as intended by the parties. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction,' where a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section. Scraped Law Snippet 1 refers to Section 431.080, which, according to the LLM explanation, supports the interpretation of contracts reasonably and fairly. By obscuring the 'Equitable Relief' clause, the changed text hinders the fair and reasonable interpretation of the contract and contradicts the general intent behind Section 431.080, as outlined in the Law Explanation.
